sentence: go to en.wikipedia.org, search for a topic, and if a disambiguation page appears pick the first listed meaning, otherwise just read the article

workflow.name: On Browser Home Page
workflow.description: Start the process from a fresh browser tab.
triggers: If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/

workflow.name: On Wikipedia Homepage
workflow.description: Search for a specific topic on Wikipedia.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
If a search box with placeholder 'Search Wikipedia' is visible

workflow.name: On Disambiguation Page
workflow.description: If a disambiguation page appears, click the first listed meaning.
triggers: If the page title contains 'disambiguation'
If a heading with text 'Disambiguation' is visible
actions: Click the first link within the main content area (e.g., the first 'a' tag inside a 'div' with class 'mw-parser-output')

workflow.name: On Article Page
workflow.description: The target article is open, either directly or after disambiguation, completing the task.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/
If the page title does NOT contain 'disambiguation'
If a heading with text 'Contents' is visible

sentence: go to en.wikipedia.org, search for a topic, and if a disambiguation page appears pick the first listed meaning, otherwise just read the article

lint_issues.message: Empty actions with a wait/verification-sounding name -- looks like an intentional 'sit here until something external changes' state. Confirm that's what you meant.
Last state's actions don't mention 'Terminate'. NetGent's web agent needs an explicit terminate instruction to stop cleanly.
